regionVocabulary.appliesTo: New-VM.ps1 across all guests/hosts (newvm-ubuntu, newvm-amazon, newvm-cacheproxy, newvm-stash, newvm-win-mac). Keep the artifact noun where it genuinely differs: 'Seek the base IPSW' (guest.macos.26), 'Required ISOs' (KVM guest.windows.11 which fetches two ISOs).

Host-specific artifact-layout wordings (KVM 'Per-VM directory + disk', macOS 'Build the UTM bundle skeleton'/'Create UTM bundle') are acceptable where the layout genuinely differs.

aliases: Create VM disk (copy of base image)
regionVocabulary.appliesTo: The disk-copy sub-step in the infra guests (newvm-cacheproxy, newvm-stash). Hyper-V 'Create VM disk (copy of base image)' and KVM 'Copy base image -> per-VM disk' name the same block; unify to 'Copy base image -> per-VM disk'. macOS folds this inside its bundle region (justified).

The seed-ISO build step across New-VM.ps1 (newvm-amazon, newvm-cacheproxy, newvm-stash). Hyper-V/UTM already agree on 'Generate cloud-init seed ISO'. KVM titles the equivalent 'Render user-data / meta-data'; where KVM performs a distinct render step AND a distinct ISO build, keep 'Render user-data / meta-data' for the render and add 'Generate cloud-init seed ISO' for the genisoimage/seed build so the seed step is named family-wide.

aliases: Build the autoinstall apt block via the shared builder

aliases: Fetch caching-proxy CA cert (base64-embedded in seed)

Trailing cleanup region in New-VM.ps1 (Hyper-V/UTM guests) and windows.11.update.ps1. Prefer 'Cleanup temporary folders' for the New-VM family; the guest-update ps1 'Clean up temporary files' describes files not folders and may keep its noun, but standardize the verb spelling to 'Clean up' vs 'Cleanup' within its own family.
Trailing operator-guidance region in New-VM.ps1 (Hyper-V/UTM guests; macOS caching-proxy). Already consistent; only divider decoration needs normalizing.

Host-precondition region, present on UTM New-VM.ps1 members (guest.ubuntu.server.24/26, guest.windows.11, guest.macos.26). Genuinely UTM-scoped; where a header exists, use singular-or-plural canonical 'Environment checks'.
KVM New-VM.ps1 and ubuntu.kvm/Enable-TestAutomation.ps1. KVM-only (justified). Keep '(self-heal)' where the block self-heals the ACL; drop the trailing dash run only.

aliases: Configuration (change these to customize the download)
regionVocabulary.appliesTo: Get-Image.ps1 (getimage-amazon, getimage-infra, getimage-win-mac). Where the parenthetical '(change these to customize the download)' exists it may stay, but the base word must be 'Configuration' everywhere.
Get-Image.ps1 infra guests (getimage-infra). Present only on Hyper-V today; add the same region above the Save-ImageWithChecksum block in UTM and KVM so all six mark the download step.
Get-Image.ps1 amazon guests (getimage-amazon). Present only on Hyper-V; add the same region above the URL-resolution code in UTM and KVM.
Get-Image.ps1 amazon guests (getimage-amazon). Add to KVM at the matching phase boundary.

In the caching-proxy family the two sub-blocks are split ('Yuruna harness SSH key' + 'Cache-VM yuruna password'); keep those two split titles for cacheproxy.

The scopes genuinely differ (justified): keep 'Wait for VM IP' where the VM was already started via virt-install --import, and 'Start VM and wait for ...' where Start-VM is inside the block.

test-services Start-/Stop-CachingProxy.ps1 numbered step scheme. Keep the numbered 'Step N:' inside the REGION title; the 'sudo preflight' variant on Stop is a genuine per-script difference (justified). Preserve the numbering; only normalize the divider decoration.

linkMarkerRule: Replace the literal prefix "# --- See " with "# --- REGION: " on any single-line comment whose content is a yuruna.link (or other) URL, preserving the line's ORIGINAL leading indentation byte-for-byte and keeping the URL, #anchor, and any trailing parenthetical (e.g. "(macos-host hosts alias)") exactly unchanged. Drop only the word "See". This applies to the ~150+ "# --- See https://yuruna.link/..." markers across guest scripts, New-VM/Get-Image drivers, vmconfig user-data, automation libs, and test services. Do NOT touch: (a) inline prose comments that merely mention a URL mid-sentence (e.g. the .26 Hyper-V/UTM apt-block where the URL is inlined in a multi-line sentence — those should instead be re-broken out into a standalone "# --- REGION: <url>" line, a structural fix), and (b) the "# === YURUNA_OVERLAY_* ===" anchor lines, which are functional merge tokens, not link/divider markers.

structuralFixes.issue: Ubuntu New-VM.ps1 pipeline steps (Build the autoinstall apt block, Fetch caching-proxy CA cert, Render user-data / meta-data, Build the seed ISO) are titled on KVM but unmarked plain code on Hyper-V/UTM. The same shared pipeline reads with a section skeleton on one host and none on the others.
structuralFixes.action: Adopt one shared REGION header set for the common pipeline steps (Seek the base image; Create copies and files for VM; Build the autoinstall apt block; Fetch caching-proxy CA cert; Render user-data / meta-data; Cleanup temporary folders; Guidance) and apply identically in all six files. Keep host-only regions (libvirt-qemu ACL / Inputs / Define+start via virt-install on KVM; Environment checks on UTM) where genuinely host-specific.

structuralFixes.issue: The .26 Hyper-V/UTM Ubuntu variants inline the apt-proxy-block yuruna.link URL inside a multi-line prose sentence instead of a standalone link marker; the .24 variants and both KVM variants keep a standalone '# --- See <url>' line. Inconsistent anchor marking.
structuralFixes.action: Re-break the apt-proxy-block URL out into a standalone '# --- REGION: https://yuruna.link/vmconfig#apt-proxy-block' line above the Build-AptProxyBlock call in the two .26 Hyper-V/UTM files so the anchor is marked uniformly across all six.

structuralFixes.issue: The KVM amazon New-VM.ps1 omits the 'Seek the base image' and 'Create copies and files for VM' section headers that Hyper-V and UTM both carry, even though it performs the same base-image-seek and disk-copy steps.
structuralFixes.action: Add '# --- REGION: Seek the base image' above the KVM base-image block and a create/seed region header, so all three amazon members share the block skeleton.

structuralFixes.issue: 'Generate cloud-init seed ISO' is a dedicated region only on UTM; Hyper-V folds the seed build into 'Create copies and files for VM' and KVM has no seed header.
structuralFixes.action: Promote 'Generate cloud-init seed ISO' to a shared region and add it where each host builds the seed, so the seed step is named family-wide.

structuralFixes.issue: Only the KVM caching-proxy file titles the SSH-key load ('Yuruna harness SSH key') and the password-persistence dance ('Cache-VM yuruna password'); Hyper-V and macOS carry the identical logic as inline prose with no section header.
structuralFixes.action: Add '# --- REGION: Yuruna harness SSH key' and '# --- REGION: Cache-VM yuruna password' dividers to the Hyper-V and macOS caching-proxy files above the matching blocks for a consistent skeleton.

structuralFixes.issue: The macOS caching-proxy file omits titled regions for the boot-disk copy and the Wi-Fi-vs-Ethernet NetworkMode decision, while Hyper-V and KVM title the disk-copy and network-pick steps.
structuralFixes.action: Add '# --- REGION: Copy base image -> per-VM disk' and '# --- REGION: Pick network mode' to the macOS caching-proxy file so its skeleton matches its siblings.

structuralFixes.issue: The existing-VM teardown ('idempotent rebuild') step is titled on KVM ('Tear down any existing VM...') but runs inline with prose on Hyper-V.
structuralFixes.action: Add '# --- REGION: Remove existing VM' above the Hyper-V teardown blocks so the shared rebuild step is titled in both hypervisors (unify the title to 'Remove existing VM').

structuralFixes.issue: The stash Hyper-V file lacks a divider before the byte-identical SSH-key/vault-password block that macOS and KVM both title 'Yuruna harness SSH key + vault password'.
structuralFixes.action: Add '# --- REGION: Yuruna harness SSH key + vault password' above the Hyper-V block (keep the existing inline rationale as following prose).

structuralFixes.issue: Hyper-V and macOS stash files carry 'Generate cloud-init seed ISO'; KVM groups render + genisoimage under 'Render user-data / meta-data' with no seed-ISO header.
structuralFixes.action: Either rename the KVM region to 'Generate cloud-init seed ISO' or split the genisoimage call out under that shared header so the family uses one consistent seed-ISO region name.

structuralFixes.issue: KVM Remove-OrphanedVMFiles.ps1 omits the 'List registered VMs and their associated files' and 'Cleanup result' section headers that Hyper-V and UTM both have, though it contains the equivalent listing loop and result summary.
structuralFixes.action: Add '# --- REGION: List registered VMs and their associated files' above the KVM print loop and '# --- REGION: Cleanup result' above the KVM summary.

structuralFixes.issue: The UTM Windows-11 Get-Image.ps1 wraps sections in three-line '# ====...' rule boxes around a '# N) Title' line; the family uses flat single-line headers.
structuralFixes.action: Collapse each three-line box to a single '# --- REGION: N. Title' line (normalize 'N)' to 'N.'), deleting the surrounding rule-only '# ====...' lines. Keep the numbered/two-artifact structure (justified).

structuralFixes.issue: Start-CachingProxy.ps1 Step 1.5 divider terminates with Unicode box-drawing dashes while every other divider in the same file uses ASCII; a lone stylistic outlier.
structuralFixes.action: Normalize to '# --- REGION: Step 1.5: ensure the yuruna-external libvirt bridge network' (no trailing run), which removes the box-drawing outlier.

structuralFixes.issue: kubeadm init is a single unretried call on ubuntu.server.24 but wrapped in a 3-attempt retry-with-reset loop on ubuntu.server.26; the two files are otherwise byte-identical up to that point. This is a hardening improvement, not a version requirement.
structuralFixes.action: Backport the ubuntu.server.26 kubeadm_init retry loop into ubuntu.server.24 so both guest k8s scripts carry the same failure-hardening. (Code/behavior change — operator decision.)

structuralFixes.issue: Both KVM Ubuntu Get-Image.ps1 calls omit '-EmitProxyDiagnosticOnFailure' that all four Hyper-V/macOS variants pass, despite KVM routing through the same squid cache. A failed cache-routed download drops the proxy diagnostic on KVM.
structuralFixes.action: Confirm intent; if unintentional, add '-EmitProxyDiagnosticOnFailure' to both KVM Save-UbuntuServerImage calls so all six drivers surface the same proxy-failure diagnostic. (Behavior change — operator decision.)

structuralFixes.issue: The Hyper-V amazon overlay runs the grubby QUIET_LOGLEVEL (and CONSOLEBLANK) command unguarded, while the KVM and UTM overlays wrap it in 'bash -c "command -v grubby ... && grubby ... || true"'. A grubby-less image would hard-fail only under Hyper-V.
structuralFixes.action: Align the Hyper-V overlay to the guarded 'command -v grubby' form used by KVM/UTM for identical degradation on a missing-grubby image. (Robustness change — operator decision.)

structuralFixes.issue: The four Hyper-V/UTM infra Get-Image.ps1 scripts hand-inline a ~10-line Last-Modified HEAD-probe + Set-Content sentinel writer, while both KVM scripts delegate to the shared Write-ImageSentinel helper. Same 4-line sentinel, duplicated logic.
structuralFixes.action: Migrate the Hyper-V/UTM scripts to call Write-ImageSentinel like KVM, removing the duplicated inline HEAD probe and its paired comment. (Code de-duplication — operator decision; also collapses long-comment candidates.)

structuralFixes.issue: The amazon Hyper-V/UTM Get-Image.ps1 hand-write a 3-line origin sentinel via Set-Content while KVM uses the newer 4-line Write-ImageSentinel (adds Last-Modified for the noble->resolute URL-bump guard). Sentinel format and re-download logic can differ between platforms.
structuralFixes.action: Confirm whether Hyper-V/UTM should migrate to Write-ImageSentinel for a uniform sentinel format; if the 3-line format is intentional, document why. (Code/behavior — operator decision.)

structuralFixes.issue: The logLevel bootstrap (Test.LogLevel import + $env:YURUNA_LOG_LEVEL honoring) is present in the macOS and KVM caching-proxy New-VM.ps1 but absent from the Hyper-V one.
structuralFixes.action: Confirm whether the Hyper-V caching-proxy script should honor logLevel; if so, add the same Test.LogLevel bootstrap. (Logic-parity — operator decision.)

structuralFixes.issue: The logLevel bootstrap and the Provenance side-channel (Test.Provenance import + Write-BaseImageProvenance) are present in the other three windows.11 New-VM.ps1 members but absent on KVM; no Linux-specific reason to omit.
structuralFixes.action: Add the logLevel import block and the Provenance import + Write-BaseImageProvenance call to the KVM windows.11 New-VM.ps1 so all four members honor $env:YURUNA_LOG_LEVEL and emit provenance. (Logic-parity — operator decision.)

structuralFixes.issue: Add-HostToPool validates the HostId (42-prefixed 32-hex regex) up front but Remove-HostFromPool takes the same parameter without validating it. Asymmetric input-validation contract.
structuralFixes.action: Decide the HostId-validation contract once and apply identically to both scripts — either both validate the format up front or neither does (relying on the membership set-diff being harmless for bad input). (Contract decision — operator.)

structuralFixes.issue: Remove-TestVMFiles.ps1 is a documented operator entry point (takes -Prefix/-Quiet) but has no comment-based help block, while its three test-vm siblings (New-LocalTestUser, Repair-CachingProxyForwarder, Test-Sequence) all provide .SYNOPSIS/.PARAMETER.
structuralFixes.action: Add a comment-based help block (.SYNOPSIS/.DESCRIPTION/.PARAMETER Prefix/.PARAMETER Quiet/.EXAMPLE); move the inline -Quiet rationale into .PARAMETER Quiet. (Documentation parity — operator decision.)

structuralFixes.issue: al2023.update.sh has a 'Network convergence' block + final 'Network ready.' repaint sentinel guarding the headless-Hyper-V idle-tail frozen-capture trap; ubuntu24/26 run apt (same transaction-tail link-bounce exposure) but carry neither the convergence block nor an end-of-script repaint sentinel.
structuralFixes.action: Consider porting the al2023 'Network convergence' + 'Network ready.' end sentinel (adapted for apt) to ubuntu24/26, or confirm those guests never hit the idle-tail capture freeze. (Behavior — operator decision.)

structuralFixes.issue: The powershell-yaml install (a HARD dependency) is hardened with a retry wrapper on the Linux+Windows guest-update scripts (pwsh_retry / for-loop) but is a single-shot 'sudo pwsh Install-Module' on macos.26, making it fragile to a transient PSGallery blip.
structuralFixes.action: Consider giving macos.26 a retry (mirror the windows.11 for-loop or a curl_retry-style wrapper), or confirm single-shot PSGallery reliability is acceptable. (Behavior — operator decision.)

longCommentMoves.topic: Skip-if-same-source guard: what Test-DownloadAlreadyCurrent checks (filename/URL/byte-count/Last-Modified), the 4-line sentinel, and the noble->resolute URL-bump silent-skip regression class

Why the Last-Modified HEAD probe is captured after download and how Test-DownloadAlreadyCurrent compares it (tolerating a missing header). Duplicated verbatim across the four Hyper-V/UTM infra Get-Image scripts.
macOS/UTM qcow2-vs-raw correctness: F_PUNCHHOLE APFS 4 KiB alignment EINVAL, why qcow2 is required (references feedback_macos-qemu-punchhole-alignment.md)
VM memory/vCPU sizing rationale (12 GB / 4 vCPU sized around squid cache_mem 9 GB) — near-identical block repeated across all three caching-proxy New-VM scripts
yuruna user password persistence model (vault + <track>/yuruna-caching-proxy.yml source-of-truth, 1/2/3 order-of-operations) — duplicated across the caching-proxy family
networkStorage pool replicate + pool auth token + Host Config Service mTLS baking rationale — three near-identical multi-line blocks repeated verbatim across all three caching-proxy files
Fetch caching-proxy CA cert / why an empty CaCertBase64 is not a harmless no-op (curl rc=60 SSL-bump gate)
Hyper-V shared-ISO DACL bloat from orphaned per-VM ACEs hitting the 64KB cap (0x8007053C); why kept base images get stale ACEs pruned every run

Why daemon.json is written before docker-ce install and registry-mirrors routes docker.io through the zot pull-through cache (stale-on-error masks ECR Public 400 blips), plus CACHE_HOST derivation — duplicated between the two Ubuntu k8s scripts

Why Flannel is fetched from the in-tree Documentation/kube-flannel.yml at the releases/latest-redirect tag (assetless-release 404 / shared-NAT 403), not releases/latest/download or api.github.com — duplicated between the two Ubuntu k8s scripts

OpenTofu deb-primary/standalone-fallback rationale: inner fetches wrapped in _yuruna_retry, pin --opentofu-version to avoid api.github.com latest 403 on shared NAT, command -v tofu guard — duplicated between the two Ubuntu k8s scripts

Snapshot-cache + cache_mem + container-registry-digest tuning rationale (objects until near-full, offline_mode replay, cache_replacement_policy ordering, cache_mem budget math, digest-pinned immutable manifest caching)

Stash Service daemon overview + storage layout (share vs VM-local, local-fallback non-durability) — duplicates the yuruna.link/stash-service doc already pointed to on line 21; shorten to a one-line summary plus the pointer, keep the top-of-file banner
Why guest-update scripts install pwsh early and pre-extract the yuruna framework (host failure diagnostic shells back as pwsh; Get-SystemDiagnostic must be on disk before any later stall)
Why wait for network convergence before signaling script-done (DHCP bounce at package-transaction tail vs first host->guest SSH / Hyper-V External vSwitch ARP-discovery trap)

Grafana zot-dashboard rewriter step-3/5.x rationale (drop panels for metrics zot v2.x omits, orphan-row prune, heatmap format normalize, $__rate_interval, multi+all equality->regex) — large inline block, service-specific

Embedded Swift VZMacOSInstaller/VZMacOSRestoreImage helper contract (load IPSW, config, aux storage, disk, machine id, install, ERROR_KIND stderr protocol, defer/deadlock rationale) — deep implementation detail tightly coupled to the inline Swift source

Windows detached-server launch rationale (write server script to file + launch with -File not base64 -EncodedCommand due to CreateProcess 32K cmdline limit; explicit stdio redirection so a grandchild inheriting console handles does not pin the outer runner)

summary:
## Yuruna comment/region normalization — global plan

**Marker inventory.** Across the 27 families the discovery pass found roughly **330+ normalizable markers**: ~150 link markers (`# --- See https://yuruna.link/...`, dominated by `caching-proxy.base.user-data` alone at ~65, plus `vmconfig` user-data files, the guest `.sh` scripts, `Get-Image`/`New-VM` drivers, `automation/*.sh`, and `test/Start-*.ps1`), and ~180 divider/numbered section headers (`# === X ===`, `# -- X ----`, `#### X ####`, `# --- X`) concentrated in the `Yuruna.Host.psm1` modules, `Start-StatusService.ps1` (~28), `Test-Sequence.ps1` (~23), the `Remove-OrphanedVMFiles`/`New-VM`/`Get-Image` drivers, and the Windows `.ps1` guest scripts.

**Two mechanical rules cover the vast majority.**
1. Link markers: `# --- See <url>` → `# --- REGION: <url>` (drop "See", keep URL/anchor/indent exactly).
2. Dividers: strip surrounding/trailing `=`/`-`/`#` runs and the word decorations → `# --- REGION: Title`, preserving leading indentation and the language comment char (`#`). Numbered dividers keep their number, normalizing `N)`→`N.`.

**Critical exclusion (verified in code).** The `# === YURUNA_OVERLAY_* ===` lines in all 12 `host/vmconfig/*.base.user-data` and `*.overlay.yml` files are **functional merge tokens**, matched literally by `Read-OverlaySection`/`Merge` in `automation/Yuruna.CloudInitTemplate.psm1` via regex `^\s*#\s*===\s*YURUNA_OVERLAY_([A-Z0-9_]+)\s*===\s*$`. Normalizing them to `# --- REGION:` would break base↔overlay injection across every hypervisor. **Leave them verbatim.** Also excluded: bash `echo -e "==== X ===="` banners (runtime OUTPUT, not comments), `Write-Output "== Step 5 =="` banners, shebangs, `set -euo pipefail`, shellcheck/PSScriptInfo/LICENSEURI/Copyright/Version headers, and here-string content.

**Families with the most drift.** (a) **Ubuntu `New-VM.ps1`** (6 files) — KVM titles the whole seed pipeline, Hyper-V/UTM leave it as plain code; largest structural-skeleton gap. (b) **`Yuruna.Host.psm1`** (3 files) — 8 shared contract sections need identical REGION titles (`Module setup`, `Image`, `VM I/O`, `Discovery`, `Networking`, `Caching proxy`, `Host config`, `Exports`). (c) **infra/amazon `Get-Image.ps1`** — KVM omits `Configuration`/`Find the file`/`Download`/`Resize` headers the others carry. (d) **`Remove-OrphanedVMFiles.ps1`** — KVM missing `List registered VMs...` and `Cleanup result`. (e) the big single files `Start-StatusService.ps1` and `Test-Sequence.ps1` are self-consistent and only need style normalization.

**Top language-standardization decisions.** Pick ONE wording per recurring block: `Seek the base image` (not "Locate base image"); `Cleanup temporary folders` (New-VM family); `Copy base image -> per-VM disk`; `Generate cloud-init seed ISO`; `Remove existing VM` (drop "with the same name"); `Environment checks`; `Yuruna harness SSH key + vault password`. Where the artifact genuinely differs the noun stays (`Seek the base IPSW`, `Required ISOs`, `Convert qcow2 to VHDX + resize` vs `Resize the qcow2 to <N> GB`).

**Recommended implementation batches (ordered by risk).**
- **Batch 1 (mechanical, low risk, comment-only):** apply the two REGION rules everywhere EXCEPT the `YURUNA_OVERLAY_*` tokens. Pure text; no behavior change. Do this first and run PSScriptAnalyzer per subdir after.
- **Batch 2 (language unification, low risk):** rename drifted region titles to the `regionVocabulary` canonicals; re-break the two `.26` Hyper-V/UTM inlined apt-block URLs into standalone REGION lines.
- **Batch 3 (structural skeleton parity, low risk):** add the missing shared REGION headers listed in structuralFixes (KVM seed pipeline, KVM `Remove-OrphanedVMFiles`, UTM/KVM `Get-Image` phases, Hyper-V/macOS SSH-key & password regions), collapse the UTM Win11 `# ====` boxes.
- **Batch 4 (long-comment→docs consolidation, low risk):** move/shorten the de-duplicated rationale (skip-if-same-source guard, VM sizing, password persistence, Flannel/OpenTofu/containerd-mirror, Last-Modified probe) into `docs/vmconfig.md`, `docs/network.md`, `docs/caching-proxy.md`, `docs/definition.md` under the anchors in longCommentMoves; leave one REGION-link line per site.
- **Batch 5 (behavior/logic parity, medium risk — operator review, NOT comment-only):** kubeadm-init retry backport to ubuntu.server.24.k8s; `-EmitProxyDiagnosticOnFailure` on KVM `Get-Image`; guarded `command -v grubby` in the Hyper-V amazon overlay; `Write-ImageSentinel` migration for Hyper-V/UTM `Get-Image`; logLevel/Provenance parity on KVM windows.11 `New-VM`; al2023 network-convergence sentinel to ubuntu24/26 update scripts; powershell-yaml retry on macos.26; HostId-validation contract for Add/Remove-HostFromPool.
